title: Module 1
content:
Fundamentals of paint science: reflection, refraction, diffraction Paint
components, pigments, pigment properties, different types, pigment
dispersion methods, solvents, different types, solvent properties, oils, driers,
additives

title: Module 2
content:
Classification based on polymeric resin - emulsion, oil and alkyd paints,
acrylic paints, epoxy coatings, polyurethanes, silicones, chlorinated rubbers,
vinyl coatings, Classification based on application - appliance finishes,
automotive finishes, coil coatings, can coatings, marine coatings,
aircraft finishes.

title: Module 3
content:
Mechanism of film formation and drying: - factors affecting coating
properties, film thickness, film density, internal stresses, pigment volume
concentration (PVC). Mechanical and optical properties of coatings, ageing
properties, effect of rheological behaviour on paint performance.

title: Module 4
content:
Adhesion properties of coatings: -factors affecting adhesive bond,
thermodynamics of adhesion, testing of paint materials- destructive and non-
destructive methods, paint film defects, surface cleaning treatments,
paint application methods.
Project based learning (8 hrs)
1. On the first class before starting the first module, direct the students to prepare a polymer-based
paint formulation
2. Students shall refer water based and solvent based paint formulations of different companies.
3. Analyze the importance of different components in a paint formulation
4. Analyze the different additives and its importance based on their function.
5. Design a suitable equipment for pigment dispersion and compare different pigment dispersion
methods.
Manufacture water and solvent based paints and study different mechanical properties using
various testing methods after the completion of all 4 modules.
6.
Prepare a project report based on their project work.
